fault_hypothesis: The overlap condition on line 69 uses strict inequalities (`c_end <= start` and `c_start >= end`), which incorrectly excludes shared-endpoint cases. It should use strict inequalities in the opposite sense: `c_end < start or c_start > end` to implement a closed-interval model where shared endpoints count as overlapping.

spec.expr: not (c_end__1 < start or c_start__1 > end) == (not (c_end__1 <= start or c_start__1 >= end) or c_end__1 == start or c_start__1 == end)

constraints.intent: When endpoints are shared (c_end==start or c_start==end), the strict-inequality condition should still detect overlap but the current <= / >= condition does not.
When c_end equals start, the condition c_end <= start is True (no overlap detected), but the closed-interval model requires overlap to be detected.
When c_start equals end, the condition c_start >= end is True (no overlap detected), but the closed-interval model requires overlap to be detected.

spec.expr: (c_end__1 < start or c_start__1 > end) == (c_end__1 <= start or c_start__1 >= end) or c_end__1 == start or c_start__1 == end

constraints.intent: At the end of each iteration, the strict and non-strict conditions differ only at shared endpoints; the bug manifests when endpoints are equal.
